title.en: pathlib & File Operations: Modern File Handling
title.ta: pathlib & கோப்பு செயல்பாடுகள்: நவீன கோப்பு கையாளுதல்

description.en: Use pathlib.Path for cross-platform file operations — glob, reading, writing, directory traversal, and replacing os.path.
description.ta: pathlib.Path பயன்படுத்தி cross-platform கோப்பு செயல்பாடுகள் — glob, reading, writing, directory traversal கற்றுக்கொள்ளுங்கள்.

story_variants.en:
## Vaathiyaar's File Cabinet

Vaathiyaar opens a modern digital filing cabinet. "The old `os.path` way was clunky — joining paths with strings, checking existence with separate functions. `pathlib` gives you an **object-oriented** approach where paths are objects, not strings!"

### pathlib vs os.path

| Task | os.path (old) | pathlib (modern) |
|------|--------------|------------------|
| Join paths | `os.path.join('a', 'b')` | `Path('a') / 'b'` |
| Check exists | `os.path.exists(p)` | `p.exists()` |
| Read file | `open(p).read()` | `p.read_text()` |
| List dir | `os.listdir(p)` | `p.iterdir()` |
| Glob | `glob.glob('*.py')` | `p.glob('*.py')` |

### Path Object Properties

```python
from pathlib import Path
p = Path('/home/user/project/main.py')
print(p.name)    # main.py
print(p.stem)    # main
print(p.suffix)  # .py
print(p.parent)  # /home/user/project
```

> **Key Concept:** Use the `/` operator with Path objects to join paths. It works on all operating systems — no more worrying about \ vs /!

explanation.en: Import Path — the star of pathlib
Use / operator to build paths — clean and cross-platform
project/src — the directory containing the file
Get the current working directory as a Path object
Create a Path object for a new directory
Create the directory — exist_ok=True means no error if it exists
Build path to a file inside the directory
Write text directly — no need for open/close!
Read entire file contents as string
Glob pattern matching — find all .py files

instruction.en: Create a directory 'notes', write 3 text files into it ('note1.txt', 'note2.txt', 'note3.txt'), then use glob to find all .txt files and print their contents.
instruction.ta: 'notes' directory உருவாக்கி, 3 text files எழுதி, glob பயன்படுத்தி அனைத்து .txt files கண்டுபிடித்து contents அச்சிடுங்கள்.

practice_challenges.starter_code:
from pathlib import Path

notes_dir = Path('notes')
notes_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)

for i in range(1, 4):
    (notes_dir / f'note{i}.txt').write_text(f'This is note {i}')

for f in notes_dir.glob('*.txt'):
    print(f'{f.name}: {f.read_text()}')
practice_challenges.expected_output:
note1.txt: This is note 1
note2.txt: This is note 2
note3.txt: This is note 3

en: Use dir.glob('*.txt') to find all txt files
Use f.read_text() to read each file

q.en: How do you join paths using pathlib?
Which method reads an entire file as a string?
q.ta: எந்த முறை முழு கோப்பையும் சரமாக படிக்கும்?
